q: 1. Mi Mi wasn't paying attention to the teacher. She didn't understand the lesson. (Join the sentences, using 'as a result'.) 2026
a: Mi Mi wasn't paying attention to the teacher, and as a result, she didn't understand the lesson.

q: 2. The weather was bad. The outdoor event was canceled. (Join the sentences, using the linking word 'as a consequence'.) 2026
a: The weather was bad, and as a consequence, the outdoor event was canceled.

q: 3. He died last week. He died of complications from cancer. (Join the sentences, using 'as a result of'.) 2026
a: He died last week as a result of complications from cancer.

q: 4. Sandi was awarded first prize in the essay competition. She was praised by her friends. (Join the sentences, using 'as a result of'.) 2025
a: Sandi was praised by her friends as a result of being awarded the first prize in the essay competition.

q: 5. She failed to submit the assignment on time. She received a lower grade. (Join the sentences using 'as a result'.) 2025
a: She failed to submit the assignment on time. As a result, she received a lower grade. (OR) She failed to submit the assignment on time, and as a result, she received a lower grade.

q: 6. Thet Htar did not have time to study for the test. She did not do very well. (Join the sentences, using "consequently") 2025
a: Thet Htar did not have time to study for the test. Consequently, she did not do very well.

q: 7. It rained continuously. The city experienced flooding. (Join the sentences using 'as a result of.) 2025
a: It rained continuously, and as a result, the city experienced flooding.

q: 8. The picnic was ruined. It started raining suddenly. (Join the sentences, using 'as a result of'.) 2026
a: The picnic was ruined as a result of it starting to rain suddenly.
